entries.source:
Chat Edit: '**Validation Checklist — Verify these before claiming 15x speedup:**

**1. Socket Mode Verification (Critical)**
Your ASM `select()` loop assumes non-blocking sockets. Verify initialization sets:
```cpp
u_long mode = 1; // Non-blocking
ioctlsocket(clientSocket, FIONBIO, &mode);
ioctlsocket(targetSocket, FIONBIO, &mode);
```
Blocking sockets will hang in `recv()` regardless of ASM optimization.

**2. Context Pool Exhaustion Handler**
Your ASM returns `-1` when `MAX_CONCURRENT_RELAYS` (1024) hits. Add this fallback in C++:
```cpp
// In relayConnection() fallback
if (ctx < 0) {
    // ASM pool full, fall back to C++ thread (degraded but functional)
    std::thread(relayConnectionCPP, clientSocket, targetSocket, entry).detach();
}
```

**3. Hotpatch Activation Code**
You mentioned "hotpatch support" but didn't show the trigger. Here's the C++ integration to dynamically instrument live connections:

```cpp
// In Win32IDE_NetworkPanel.cpp - Add to cmdNetworkAddPort():
void* targetAddr = (void*)&portForwardWorker; // Function to intercept
void* sniperAddr = (void*)&TrafficInspectorSniper; // Your inspector

// Install snipe at runtime
_asm {
    mov rcx, targetAddr
    mov rdx, sniperAddr
    call NetworkInstallSnipe
}
```

**4. ASM Test Harness (Verify Integration)**
Create `test_network_relay.asm` to validate the pool allocator under load:

```asm
; =============================================================================
; Test Network Relay Integration
; =============================================================================
INCLUDE ksamd64.inc

EXTRN RelayEngine_Init:PROC
EXTRN RelayEngine_CreateContext:PROC
EXTRN RelayEngine_GetStats:PROC

.data
testEntry STRUCT
    bytesTransferred DQ 0
    running DB 1
    padding DB 7 DUP(0)
testEntry ENDS

entryData testEntry <>

.code
main PROC
    sub rsp, 40
    
    ; Test 1: Init pool
    mov rcx, 67108864 ; 64MB
    call RelayEngine_Init
    test rax, rax
    jnz @@fail_init
    
    ; Test 2: Create 1024 contexts (max pool)
    xor r12, r12
@@alloc_loop:
    cmp r12, 1024
    jae @@test_stats
    
    mov rcx, r12 ; fake socket handle
    mov rdx, r12 
    lea r8, entryData
    call RelayEngine_CreateContext
    
    cmp rax, -1
    je @@fail_full
    
    inc r12
    jmp @@alloc_loop
    
@@test_stats:
    ; Verify stats read
    lea rcx, entryData
    lea rdx, [rsp + 24] ; temp buffer
    call RelayEngine_GetStats
    
    ; Success
    xor ecx, ecx
    call ExitProcess
    
@@fail_init:
    mov ecx, 1
    call ExitProcess
    
@@fail_full:
    ; Should not fail before 1024
    mov ecx, 2
    call ExitProcess
main ENDP
END
```

**5. Performance Verification Script**
Run this PowerShell to measure actual throughput vs C++ baseline:
```powershell
# Network stress test
$port = 3000
$bytes = 0
$stopwatch = [System.Diagnostics.Stopwatch]::StartNew()

# Create dummy TCP server
$listener = [System.Net.Sockets.TcpListener]::new([System.Net.IPAddress]::Loopback, $port)
$listener.Start()

# Connect through your forwarded port
$client = [System.Net.Sockets.TcpClient]::new("localhost", $port)
$stream = $client.GetStream()
$payload = New-Object byte[] (65536)

while ($stopwatch.ElapsedMilliseconds -lt 10000) {
    $stream.Write($payload, 0, $payload.Length)
    $bytes += $payload.Length
}

$mbps = ($bytes * 8 / 1MB) / ($stopwatch.ElapsedMilliseconds / 1000)
Write-Host "Throughput: $([math]::Round($mbps, 2)) Mbps"

# Expected: >9,000 Mbps (10GbE saturation) vs ~600 Mbps C++ baseline
```

**Critical Bug Watch:**
Your ASM uses `lock add QWORD PTR [rcx], rdx` for byte counters. If `PortForwardEntry` isn't 8-byte aligned, this will #GP fault. Verify:
```cpp
alignas(64) struct PortForwardEntry { ... }; // Matches ASM large-page alignment
```

**Next Phase:** Implement the pre-parser sniping for traffic inspection by hooking `recv()` return paths in the ASM to scan for HTTP headers before relay.'
